item.title: ‘Let us wail!’ Iranians flock to Khamenei’s funeral after wartime death
item.description: Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Khamenei, who was killed in February by the opening airstrikes of the war launched by the U.S. and Israel, in a show of public devotion to the Islamic Republic’s theocratic state and revolutionary zeal.

item.title: PM vows proper recognition of July martyrs, rehabilitation of injured
item.description: The prime minister reaffirmed his government’s commitment to bringing those responsible for the killings during the July uprising to justice but stressed that due process must prevail.

item.title: July martyrs’ families become emotional getting PM at side
item.description: Representatives of the martyrs’ families also presented a commemorative plaque to Prime Minister Tarique Rahman.

item.title: Govt revises honorarium rates for recruitment, promotion examinations
item.description: The circular also revised the daily honorarium for officials and employees directly involved in conducting examinations based on their grades.

item.title: Gas reserves in Bangladesh’s producing fields shrink to 6 Tcf
item.description: Beyond the producing fields, the rest are abandoned or idle. Though their volumes remain on the books, the fields have sat unused for years.

item.title: Speaker pays tribute at state funeral of Iran’s late Supreme Leader
item.description: During the programme, Hafiz Uddin Ahmad met Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and other senior leaders and signed the condolence book.

item.title: China launches coast guard patrol east of Taiwan despite international pushback
item.description: The fleet will conduct “law enforcement patrols” in the area, China’s Coast Guard said in a statement.

item.title: Iran exploring oil sales to Japan, buyers seek longer sanctions waiver, sources say
item.description: Three Japanese buyers were looking at possible crude oil purchases from Iran, their first since 2019, ​said two Iranian sources.

item.title: America at 250 celebrating freedom, friendship, and the future with Bangladesh
item.description: No description available

item.title: I hope Bangladesh’s trade with the United States will gradually increase
item.description: No description available

item.title: NATO leaders to gather in Ankara, aiming to smooth over tensions with Trump
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte says ‌the gathering next Tuesday and Wednesday will show Europeans are honouring pledges to hike defence spending to deter Russia from any attack, with arms deals worth tens of billions of dollars to be signed.

item.title: Khamenei lies in state in Tehran as Iran begins week of funeral ceremonies
item.description: Iran is staging a week of mass funeral processions for Khamenei, whose 37-year reign was brought to an end in February ‌by the first airstrike of the war, in a show of public devotion to the Islamic Republic’s theocratic state and revolutionary zeal.

item.title: Immediate action against any attempt to disrupt law and order: Water Resources Minister
item.description: About the drug menace, the minister alleged that some influential people were involved in drug trafficking and urged them to abandon the illegal trade.

item.title: Govt to reduce fuel price gradually: State Minister
item.description: The state minister said that the government has reduced the LPG cylinder price in line with the global situation.

item.title: Mikel Oyarzabal’s 2 goals send dominant Spain past Austria 3-0 and into round of 16 at World Cup
item.description: When the knockout rounds finally arrived Thursday, this star-studded team turned on its full talent and served notice that Spain can be just as good as everybody thought — and it’s probably getting better.

item.title: Ramos, Ronaldo score as Portugal rallies to beat Croatia 2-1 and advance to round of 16
item.description: The game featured a matchup of 40-somethings — Ronaldo, in his sixth World Cup, and Croatia’s Luka Modrić, making his fifth bid for a tournament title.

item.title: Five more children die in 24 hours with Measles symptoms
item.description: From March 15 to July 3, a total of 729 children have died across the country due to measles and its symptoms, according to the DGHS data.

item.title: Strong media indispensable for vibrant democracy: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister said politicians are not above criticism and that constructive scrutiny by the media plays a vital role in ensuring accountability.

item.title: Bangladesh risks losing its second-place position in global apparel exports
item.description: Analysts say that without rapid improvements in production capacity, market diversification, value addition, and the investment environment, Bangladesh’s position in the global ranking could face even tougher challenges.

item.title: State minister attends certificate awarding ceremony at Foreign Service Academy
item.description: The state minister underscored the importance of developing a highly skilled and professional diplomatic corps capable of effectively advancing Bangladesh’s national interests under the “Bangladesh First” policy.

item.title: Education Minister to stop teachers’ participation in local polls
item.description: The minister said teachers will remain in the teaching profession, and if they participate in the elections, they will go into politics — if the two run together, the quality of education will be diminished.

item.title: Palestinian ambassador calls on PM’s adviser on foreign affairs
item.description: The Palestinian ambassador expressed interest in further strengthening the existing friendly relations between the two countries by expanding cooperation in areas such as trade, education, culture and people-to-people exchanges.

item.title: China and Bangladesh explore direct connectivity, economic corridor
item.description: Chinese Ambassador Yao Wen briefed reporters in Dhaka on the outcomes of Prime Minister Tarique Rahman’s June 22-26 visit to China.

item.title: Sand worth BDT 3.5 billion missing from Gorai River dredging project
item.description: Local residents allege that, over the years, influential political figures, local vested-interest groups and dishonest syndicates exploited administrative weaknesses to remove the stockpiled sand illegally.

item.title: Youth ministry considers specialised bank for young entrepreneurs
item.description: A Department of Youth Development meeting on May 7 decided to examine the feasibility of the Youth Bank and to review the policies, financing structure and administrative framework needed to run it.

item.title: BAJUS raises gold, silver prices citing pure gold prices
item.description: The association said in a morning notice that the increase took effect from 10 am, lifting the price of a bhori of 22-carat gold to BDT 224,182 including VAT.

item.title: HSC, equivalent exams begin under CCTV surveillance
item.description: More than 1.27 million students are sitting this year’s HSC and equivalent examinations as authorities deploy centralised CCTV monitoring and body-worn police cameras.

item.title: Record $35.56bn remittance inflows in FY2025-26
item.description: The central bank data show that from December 2025 through May 2026, inflows topped $3 billion every month and touched $3.75 billion in March.

item.title: US, Iran talks conclude in Doha, focused on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The next meeting will take place after funeral processions for ​Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Qatar’s Foreign Ministry said.

item.title: The US, Canada and Mexico begin bumpy negotiations to renew North American trade pact
item.description: The regional trade pact — the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement or USMCA — that Trump negotiated and boasted about came up for renewal Wednesday, starting a process that is likely to last months, maybe longer.

item.title: US beats Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to advance to round of 16 and keep its World Cup dreams alive
item.description: Star Christian Pulisic had a goal disallowed for offside in the 78th minute and Tillman helped seal the win when he scored on a free kick from just outside the box in the 82nd with a shot off a hand of goalkeeper Nikola Vasilj.

item.title: Belgium comes back from two goals down to beat Senegal 3-2 in extra time at World Cup
item.description: Tielemans completed Belgium’s comeback by converting a penalty in the 125th minute of the game – the latest goal in World Cup history.

item.title: Harry Kane sends England into the round of 16 of the World Cup after 2-1 win against Congo
item.description: That team’s next match will be played at the Azteca Stadium, the site of Argentina great Diego Maradona’s infamous “Hand of God” goal that eliminated England from the 1986 World Cup quarterfinals.

item.title: Moroccan ambassador calls on state minister for foreign affairs
item.description: The meeting reaffirmed the longstanding friendship and growing bilateral relations between Bangladesh and Morocco.

item.title: US and Iran enter technical talks to secure peace deal, shipping restart
item.description: The indirect talks, mediated by Qatar and Pakistan, began on Tuesday night and were continuing on Wednesday, the Iranian official ​said.

item.title: State minister reiterates zero tolerance against terrorism
item.description: The state minister said that the government remains steadfast in its determination to confront terrorism and violent extremism in all spheres, adding that such forces have no place in Bangladesh and will never be allowed to undermine the country’s peace and stability.

item.title: Govt to focus on textile education, research: Education Minister
item.description: The minister said there is no alternative to producing skilled textile engineers and researchers to take Bangladesh to the top position in global competition.

item.title: CCGP approves import of 75,000 tonnes of MOP fertiliser
item.description: The fertiliser will be procured by the Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation (BADC) under G-to-G arrangements.

item.title: One more child dies with Measles symptoms
item.description: The death toll from measles and Measles symptoms now stands at 719.

item.title: Unchecked AI progress may pose catastrophic risks, UN panel warns
item.description: In the near term, it expects a shift towards agentic AI systems capable of carrying ​out real-world tasks, although growth may be constrained by energy and high-quality data shortages.

item.title: NICAR approves three new upazilas, one thana
item.description: The approvals were given at the 121st meeting of NICAR at the Cabinet Division in the Secretariat, with Prime Minister Tarique Rahman in the chair.

item.title: Central bank’s new monetary policy prioritises inflation control
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has decided to maintain its contractionary monetary policy stance to ease inflationary pressures and ensure long-term price stability.

item.title: Cybersecurity more important than “Sindhuk”: Info Minister
item.description: The information minister said, “War is not just about weapons and communication is not just about exchanging people’s voices.”

item.title: Private vehicles could face ‘congestion charge’ on Dhaka roads
item.description: The charge forms part of the Updating the Revised Strategic Transport Plan (URSTP) for Dhaka, which says a reliable public transport alternative must be in place before the levy takes effect.

item.title: Haaland scores deciding goal as Norway wins its 1st World Cup knockout game, beating Ivory Coast 2-1
item.description: A slight tap from Haaland’s left foot in the 86th minute was the difference as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on Tuesday, setting up another World Cup match against five-time champion Brazil.

item.title: Mbappé scores twice to tie Messi for World Cup lead with 6 goals, France beats Sweden 3-0
item.description: France’s 27-year-old star scored the go-ahead goal with a brilliant crossover step in the 45th minute and added his sixth goal of the tournament in the 74th to tie Lionel Messi for the tournament scoring lead.

item.title: Chittagong port lifts cargo volumes, cuts vessel turnaround time
item.description: The fall in average turnaround time for container vessels was reflected in improved operational efficiency, according to the data.

item.title: Mexico ends a 40-year knockout drought, beats Ecuador 2-0 to reach the round of 16
item.description: Quiñones opened the scoring in the 22nd minute and Jiménez added a goal in the 31st for the Mexicans, who had not won a knockout-stage match since defeating Bulgaria in the round of 16 when they hosted the tournament in 1986.

item.title: Trump reports over $1.4 billion in income from crypto ventures
item.description: Although crypto is by far the largest driver of income for Trump, his traditional businesses — in particular golf courses and resorts — continued to bring in millions.

item.title: Iran says it won’t meet with US envoys, clouding prospects for peace deal
item.description: Iranian officials also said the two sides must still ‌sort out the terms of a ceasefire they signed two weeks ago before they could tackle more difficult topics.

item.title: Parliament passes Gambling Prevention Act
item.description: The act introduces 14 categories of punishment.

item.title: Government keeps fuel prices unchanged for July
item.description: The unchanged prices will take effect from July 1.

item.title: Two more children die with Measles symptoms
item.description: In total, 625 children have died with measles-like symptoms nationwide since March 15, according to the DGHS report, while 93 children have died after being confirmed to have contracted measles.

item.title: Parliament passes budget for FY 2026–27
item.description: The new budget will come into effect on Wednesday.

item.title: Sarwar Hossain appointed deputy governor of Bangladesh Bank
item.description: Md Sarwar Hossain has been appointed as deputy governor under Article 10(4) of the Bangladesh Bank Order, 1972.

item.title: VEON Group delegation calls on BB governor
item.description: The VEON Group briefed Bangladesh Bank on its interest and plans to expand digital financial services in Bangladesh.

item.title: Australian high commissioner calls on BB governor
item.description: The meeting focused on Bangladesh’s recent macroeconomic developments, ongoing reforms in the banking sector, non-performing loan management, foreign exchange reserves, continued collaboration with international development partners, and various initiatives undertaken in response to the global economic environment.

item.title: Speaker Hafiz Uddin Ahmad to attend Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral
item.description: The speaker will attend the funeral at the invitation of Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, speaker of the Iranian Parliament.

item.title: After an earthquake, how long can trapped victims survive?
item.description: Trapped victims are more likely to survive if they are in a debris-free pocket that prevents major injury while they await rescue, like under a sturdy desk, said geophysicist Victor Tsai from Brown University.

item.title: US working on ban targeting Chinese energy inverters, sources say
item.description: The rule being drafted by the U.S. Federal Communications ‌Commission would apply to new foreign models of inverters and could be published as early as this year.

item.title: US declaration to exit USMCA to start a decade-long countdown for the pact
item.description: Trade chiefs from the U.S., Mexico and Canada are expected to meet virtually on Wednesday and declare whether they ​want to extend the pact for another 16 years.

item.title: Supreme Court to rule on Trump bid to limit birthright citizenship
item.description: Challengers to Trump’s order argued that ‌it violates language in the U.S. Constitution’s 14th Amendment that confers citizenship to those born in the United States who are “subject to the jurisdiction thereof.”

item.title: Publishing Hasina’s speech prohibited, media must follow court’s instructions: PM’s Adviser
item.description: The prime minister’s information and broadcasting adviser cited a court restriction on publishing Sheikh Hasina’s speech.

item.title: Inu convicted of crimes against humanity, jailed for 10 years
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal found the JSD president guilty on three charges linked to the killing of six protesters in Kushtia during the July 2024 uprising.

item.title: Non-stop trains bear brunt of rising stone-pelting attacks
item.description: Trains operated by the Eastern Zone have been targeted in 224 stone-pelting incidents over the past 17 months, according to Bangladesh Railway sources. Four of the five trains most frequently targeted are non-stop services.

item.title: Bangladesh’s first direct ASI recruitment draws muted response
item.description: The weak response to a mid-level post in one of the country’s most vital law-enforcement agencies, analysts note, is not merely a recruitment shortfall; it freshly exposes the profession’s current standing and appeal among young people.

item.title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro supplier list, parts and photos exposed in Tata data leak
item.description: Tata, which both supplies parts and assembles iPhones as a contract manufacturer, is emerging as ​one of Apple’s most important manufacturing partners outside China.

item.title: A-Challan mandatory for all govt receipts from July 1
item.description: Under the manual system, citizens often had to deposit challans at designated branches of selected banks, mainly Bangladesh Bank or Sonali Bank.

item.title: Central bank caps banks’ lending-deposit spread at 4%
item.description: A circular from the central bank mandates that the gap between average interest rates on all loans and deposits — excluding credit cards and consumer finance — must not exceed 4 percent.

item.title: Gold prices fall for second straight day
item.description: The latest adjustment brings the price of 22-carat gold to BDT 221,966 per bhori, inclusive of value-added tax.

item.title: Paraguay stuns Germany in a penalty shootout for the biggest upset of the 2026 World Cup
item.description: It was a major triumph for the landlocked South American country of 7 million people that’s surrounded by soccer giants like Argentina and Brazil.

item.title: Saibari’s shootout winner sends Morocco past the Netherlands, earliest World Cup exit for the Dutch
item.description: Morocco moves on to face Canada in the Round of 16 on Saturday in Houston.

item.title: Survivors decry slow aid after deadly Venezuela quakes
item.description: The rising toll of casualties - including 5,000 injured - was likely exacerbated by decades of neglect, poor building code enforcement and shoddy licensing practices.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon deal may entrench stalemate rather than end war, analysts say
item.description: At its core is a bargain few see as workable: Hezbollah has flatly rejected disarmament, ‌and no Lebanese government has the power to enforce it.

item.title: “Technology alone isn’t enough, we need to improve the process and the people too”
item.description: “To make cybersecurity tools truly effective, we need mature processes and capable people who can operate, govern, monitor and continuously improve the technology. The real value of technology comes when people and processes are able to drive it efficiently.”

item.title: Gabriel Martinelli scores late in injury time to help Brazil beat Japan 2-1 at World Cup
item.description: Martinelli holds dual citizenship in Italy and Brazil, and the man who made the decision to change the team’s makeup was Carlo Ancelotti, an Italian who is the first European to coach the South American country’s national team.

item.title: Italy and Balkans hit by heatwave as wildfire fears rise
item.description: There was also a warning that the heat was likely to build again from the start of next week in countries such as France and Germany that bore the brunt over the past few days.

item.title: National Parliament passes Finance Bill with some significant changes
item.description: Following the prime minister’s requests during his budget discussion speech, the finance minister revised several proposals in the national budget, including increasing the income tax-free threshold for individual taxpayers over the next five fiscal years.

item.title: Five more die of dengue, 172 hospitalised
item.description: The disease has claimed 18 lives so far this year.

item.title: EU reaffirms support for Bangladesh’s economic reform agenda
item.description: The EU reiterated its willingness to partner with Bangladesh in promoting rules-based trade, transparent investment, green growth and inclusive development.

item.title: Key facts on South Korea’s three chip and AI ‘mega projects’
item.description: The government said it aims for South Korea to become one of the world’s top three AI robot powers and the world’s top power ⁠in physical ​AI by 2030.

item.title: Four more children die with Measles symptoms
item.description: The latest deaths bring the total number of child fatalities from confirmed measles and measles-like symptoms to 716 over the past three and a half months.

item.title: President mourns death of eminent artist Mustafa Monwar
item.description: In a condolence message, the president prayed for the eternal peace of the departed soul and conveyed his heartfelt sympathy to the bereaved family members.

item.title: PM mourns death of eminent artist Mustafa Monwar
item.description: The prime minister said Mustafa Monwar’s death created a big vacuum in the country’s art and cultural arena that cannot be filled easily.

item.title: PM launches tree plantation campaign in secondary schools countrywide
item.description: Under the slogan “One Child, One Tree,” the prime minister simultaneously inaugurated the camping in over 29,000 secondary schools across the country.

item.title: MIDA chief calls for integrating Blue Economy into national development vision
item.description: Ashik Mahmud said the Blue Economy offers enormous opportunities to accelerate economic growth by unlocking the country’s untapped marine resources, creating millions of jobs and expanding export earnings.

item.title: NASA races to save Swift telescope from falling back to Earth with daring rescue mission
item.description: NASA hired startup Katalyst Space Technologies to boost the Swift Observatory to a higher orbit where it can continue hunting for some of the universe’s biggest explosions.

item.title: Pakistan forces kill militants in border operation, Kabul says dozens of civilians dead
item.description: Sunday’s aerial assault was Pakistan’s second on targets in Afghanistan it said belonged to militants, and threatened to exacerbate an intermittent conflict ​between the former allies.

item.title: Senior Lebanese official slams US-brokered deal with Israel, warns of divisions
item.description: Israel has occupied a swathe of southern Lebanon in a war with Hezbollah that began on March 2, ​when the group opened fire at Israel in solidarity with Tehran after it came under U.S.-Israeli attack.

item.title: Bangladesh, Pakistan seek greater cooperation in food, fertiliser, jute sectors
item.description: The minister said that stronger trade relations are essential for the sustainable economic development of both countries, adding that greater coordination between the public and private sectors would create new investment and business opportunities.

item.title: ‘Ashrayan’ welfare housing scheme loses its intended residents
item.description: A Bonik Barta investigation found that in many projects, influential local figures, intermediaries, or unauthorised occupants have taken over houses instead of the intended beneficiaries.

item.title: “Cyber education and capability development should be absolute priorities for digital security”
item.description: “Once the general public is aware, they can make informed decisions. Otherwise, no matter what tools are brought in, if people are untrained, they will still click on spam and fall for scams. So, cyber education and capability development are absolute priorities.”

item.title: Former minister Dipu Moni taken to hospital from Munshiganj jail
item.description: Sources said she became unwell suddenly at around 6 am and was rushed to the DMCH emergency department on the orders of prison authorities.

item.title: BAJUS cut gold prices by BDT 3,266
item.description: BAJUS said local pure gold prices had declined, prompting the adjustment.

item.title: Puppetry pioneer, painter Mustafa Monwar passes away at 90
item.description: Mustafa Monwar earned national recognition for his creative children’s programming on the state broadcaster BTV, which shaped the childhoods of successive generations.

item.title: Father and son rescued after four days buried under rubble of Venezuela’s earthquakes
item.description: The coastal state was hardest hit by the earthquakes on ​Wednesday that left at least 1,450 dead and thousands missing.

item.title: Iran and US agree to halt attacks and renew talks, US official says
item.description: Axios, which first reported the cessation ​of hostilities, citing a senior U.S. official, said talks would resume Tuesday in Qatar.

item.title: Feasibility study for Teesta mega project to be done quickly to begin work: Water Resources Minister
item.description: The minister said that the government has given special attention to ensuring that work on the project can begin within this budget.

item.title: New divisional commissioner appointed for Dhaka, new deputy commissioners posted to Sylhet, Cumilla
item.description: The appointments were announced on Sunday through two separate notifications issued by the Field Administration-2 Wing of the Ministry of Public Administration.

item.title: Declining rate of new entrepreneur creation worsened income inequality: Industries Minister
item.description: Referring to the global income inequality index, the minister said that the scale ranges from zero to one, with higher values indicating greater inequality, adding that Bangladesh’s level of income disparity is now much higher than before.

item.title: Students must be prepared to build prosperous Bangladesh: Prime Minister
item.description: The prime minister encouraged them to give equal importance to sports alongside their academic pursuits and to develop themselves into competent, skilled, and responsible citizens.

item.title: Final results of 47th BCS published
item.description: In total, 1,521 candidates were recommended for appointment.

item.title: Four more children die with Measles-like symptoms in 24 hours
item.description: In total, 712 children have died from confirmed measles or measles-like symptoms during the period.

item.title: Public universities lack transparent financial reporting framework
item.description: Most universities produce only basic income and expenditure statements, which fail to provide a complete picture of their financial position. Consequently, there is no clear information regarding the institutions’ total assets, liabilities, capital, or long-term financial obligations.

item.title: Pakistan HC calls on PM’s defence adviser
item.description: The Pakistani high commissioner led a three-member delegation to the office of Brigadier General (retd) Dr AKM Shamsul Islam at Dhaka Cantonment.

item.title: Bangladesh, climate leaders discuss expanding green cooperation
item.description: Both sides expressed optimism about Bangladesh’s prospects for climate-resilient development and environmentally sustainable investment.

item.title: France records 1,000 excess deaths during record-breaking heatwave
item.description: Scientists have said the heatwave, which began on ​June 20, was the worst recorded in Europe, ​where the climate is changing faster than the global average.

item.title: Bangladesh exports hatching eggs to Nigeria for first time
item.description: “Once Bangladesh can meet its own demand and export abroad, it reflects national capability,” the state minister said.

item.title: Death toll in Venezuela quake tops 1,400 as rescue efforts intensify
item.description: Officials said more than 1,600 foreign rescuers had arrived and that additional teams were on the way, adding to a growing international response.

item.title: Australia toughens kids’ social media ban, doubles potential penalties for tech firms
item.description: Australia’s six-month-old ban ⁠is being closely watched by many nations seeking to emulate it due to concerns about the impact of social media on youth mental and physical health.

item.title: South Korea, Japan reaffirm denuclearisation goal, closer defence ties
item.description: Meeting in Seoul, South Korean Defence Minister Ahn Gyu-back and his Japanese counterpart Shinjiro Koizumi agreed to work on regional stability bilaterally.

item.title: Hezbollah rejects US-brokered Israel-Lebanon security deal as ‘surrender’
item.description: The framework agreed on Friday provides for a phased Israeli withdrawal from some parts of southern Lebanon, alongside the deployment of ​the Lebanese army.

item.title: Army chief heads to Czech Republic and Slovakia on official visit
item.description: The army chief is scheduled to hold a series of meetings with senior defence officials in both countries.

item.title: Ex-DMP chief Habibur, 2 others get death for Rampura crimes against humanity
item.description: The prosecution had submitted the formal charges in the case on August 7 last year, alleging police firing on a student hanging from a building cornice in Rampura and the killing of two others in the area.

item.title: PM orders SPARRSO modernisation during surprise visit
item.description: The prime minister visited each department of the organisation and exchanged views with its officials and employees.

item.title: Messi scores in seventh straight World Cup match
item.description: Musa Al-Taamari pulled a goal back for already-eliminated Jordan in the 55th minute before Messi struck in the 80th.

item.title: US airstrikes again hit Iran as Tehran strikes Bahrain and Kuwait, further imperiling interim deal
item.description: The U.S. military’s Central Command said it struck Iranian military “surveillance infrastructure, communication systems, air defense sites, drone storage facilities and minelayer capabilities” following an attack on a ship at sea early Saturday morning.

item.title: Gas crunch leaves efficient power plants idle, raises grid losses
item.description: The 20 gas-fired plants managed by Titas, with a combined capacity of 6,076 MW, require 996 million cubic feet of gas a day to run at full capacity. But actual supply is a little over 270 million cubic feet.

item.title: Trossard, De Bruyne score and Belgium defeats New Zealand 5-1 to advance at the World Cup
item.description: The match was played simultaneously to the other group finale in Seattle between Egypt and Iran, which ended in a 1-1 draw.

item.title: Cape Verde’s dream run continues, becoming smallest country into World Cup knockout round
item.description: Cape Verde will play reigning World Cup champion Argentina in Miami on July 3.

item.title: Beijing proposes Bangladesh-China economic corridor via Myanmar
item.description: The proposal emerged during bilateral talks between Prime Minister Tarique Rahman and Chinese President Xi Jinping at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Friday.

item.title: BAJUS raises gold prices after two rounds of cuts
item.description: The association increased the price of 22‑carat gold by BDT 5,482 per bhori (11.664 grammes) to BDT 228,556.

item.title: Crucial window for rescuing survivors narrows as Venezuela enters third day after deadly twin quakes
item.description: Venezuelans took the search for missing loved ones into their own hands, citing a scarcity of government rescuers, as the human toll of Wednesday’s quakes climbed to at least 920 dead and more than 51,000 missing.

item.title: Israel, Lebanon sign initial agreement after US-mediated talks
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the agreement allows Israeli forces to continue to occupy southern Lebanon if Hezbollah does not disarm.

item.title: US strikes Iran in response to attack on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran said a projectile struck the area around a pier in Sirik in southern Iran, and that Iranian naval forces responded by striking U.S. military targets in the region.

item.title: Despite success of cooperative farming in Vietnam and India, Bangladesh made little progress
item.description: Cooperative farming could become an important instrument for modernising Bangladesh’s agricultural sector and improving rural livelihoods, according to sector insiders.

item.title: Italy regulator probes Microsoft over ‘Microsoft 365’ price hike
item.description: The regulator said the ⁠Windows maker did not ​adequately inform consumers that its ​Microsoft 365 service had been integrated with artificial intelligence tools Copilot ​and Designer.

item.title: Saudi Aramco resumes oil loading at Ras Tanura in boost to supply
item.description: Middle Eastern producers had been ramping up oil and gas output and exports in the lead-up to the interim deal between ​the United States and Iran to halt the war and reopen the strait, where a fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural ​gas supplies used to pass.

item.title: Prime Minister expresses condolences over loss of lives in Venezuela earthquake
item.description: The prime minister wished the injured a speedy recovery and expressed Bangladesh’s solidarity with Venezuela during this difficult time.

item.title: UN halts escort of ships through Hormuz after vessel comes under attack
item.description: Two U.S. officials told Reuters that Iran had fired on the ship, while Iran’s Persian Gulf Strait Authority, which Tehran established to manage requests for ships to travel through the strait, said ​vessels outside routes it has set will not be guaranteed safe passage.

item.title: ADP implementation stands at 48% in 11 months, lowest in 16 years
item.description: Despite increasing development allocations each year, the ADP implementation rate has been declining consistently over the past several years.

item.title: Nine more children die with Measles symptoms
item.description: The release further stated that 605 children have died with measles-like symptoms during the period from 15 March to 25 June, and 93 deaths have been confirmed as being caused by measles.

item.title: Tarique Rahman, Li Qiang hold bilateral talks, MoUs inked
item.description: Tarique Rahman was accorded a warm reception after he reached the Great Hall in a ceremonial motorcade from Diaoyutai State Guesthouse to join the meeting.

item.title: EU joins U.S.-led ‘Pax Silica’ on securing AI, chip supply chains
item.description: The ⁠Netherlands, earlier ​this ​week, also joined the pact.

item.title: Two powerful quakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 164 and injuring nearly 1,000
item.description: Wednesday evening’s 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes were among the strongest to strike Venezuela in more than a century and could be felt throughout the region. The country’s main airport was damaged and closed.

item.title: Bribes transactions in public services reach BDT 126.33 bn during one year of Interim Government: TIB
item.description: The organisation’s survey revealed a high prevalence of corruption in passport services, the road transport authority, law enforcement agencies, land administration, and judicial services.

item.title: Focus shifts to improving education quality: Education Minister
item.description: The minister noted that efforts are being made to identify the facilities and challenges faced by teachers, as well as the factors that are hindering educational standards, and to address those issues.

item.title: PM accorded warm reception at Great Hall in Beijing
item.description: A red carpet was rolled on the occasion as the Chinese premier cordially welcomed his Bangladeshi counterpart at the Great Hall of the People.

item.title: Minister calls for weekend customs operations to ease airport cargo backlog
item.description: Officials noted that roughly the same volume of cargo arriving each day was being cleared, but the previously accumulated stock was proving difficult to reduce.

item.title: Govt moving toward universal healthcare coverage to reduce costs: Finance Minister
item.description: The government is advancing toward a universal healthcare system to ensure preventive healthcare services at least, the minister said, adding that such a system would help increase household savings, improve public health outcomes, and provide a more secure future for children.

item.title: India to resume tourist visas for Bangladeshis from June 28
item.description: Dinesh Trivedi, who presented his credentials to Bangladesh’s President Mohammed Shahabuddin earlier in the day, said that applications for tourist visas would be accepted from June 28.

item.title: City Group debt crisis raises questions over top bankers’ judgement
item.description: City Group has applied for policy support from the central bank — a first in its 50-year history. Top executives of the banks and financial institutions that lent to the group have now united to find a solution and formed a committee to restructure the group’s debt.

item.title: Flooding in northern Bangladesh may delay Aman season
item.description: Agriculture stakeholders warn that a delayed Aman season could disrupt the entire agricultural cycle, adding that this may affect the planting and harvesting periods of various crops, including winter vegetables and the subsequent Boro rice crop.

item.title: PM calls on China’s water resources minister
item.description: The prime minister dubbed the existing bilateral cooperation in water resources management between the two countries as practical and research-based.

item.title: Bangladesh seeks stronger global support for LDCs at UN
item.description: Ambassador Salahuddin Noman Chowdhury underscored the need to safeguard the hard-earned development gains of the LDCs amid growing challenges posed by climate shocks, debt distress and the ongoing global energy crisis.

item.title: Gold prices cut for second time in 24 hours
item.description: Thursday’s adjustment brings the total number of gold price revisions this year to 81, split evenly between 40 increases and 40 cuts, with one VAT adjustment.

item.title: BNP signs first cooperation pact with Chinese Communist Party
item.description: The BNP’s joint secretary general for international relations and the minister of the CPC’s International Department signed the document on behalf of their respective parties.

item.title: PM seeks Chinese support in construction of China-Bangladesh Friendship Hospital
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman looked forward to China’s cooperation in Bangladesh’s development projects and the construction of the China-Bangladesh Friendship Hospital.

item.title: PM invites Chinese investors to co-author next great Asian economic miracle in Bangladesh
item.description: Tarique Rahman invited more Chinese companies to visit Bangladesh to see with fresh eyes as his government issues an unwavering commitment to investors.

item.title: Trump, Republican senator engage in shouting match over Iran war
item.description: Several Republicans who attended said Trump engaged in a shouting match with Senator Bill Cassidy, who said the administration needed ‌to explain a framework deal Trump signed last week.

item.title: Rescue work underway after quakes rock Venezuela, ‘high casualties’ likely
item.description: A magnitude 7.2 earthquake hit about 160 km (100 miles) west of Caracas, followed less than a minute later by a magnitude 7.5 tremor, according to the USGS.

item.title: Bangladesh repatriates 170 nationals detained in Libya
item.description: The repatriation was carried out through coordinated efforts by the foreign ministry, the Embassy of Bangladesh in Libya, and the Ministry of Expatriates’ Welfare and Overseas Employment, with the cooperation of the Libyan government and the International Organization for Migration (IOM).

item.title: Govt to prioritise welfare of ethnic minorities: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister noted that historically, the BNP has consistently worked to protect the rights of followers of all religions.

item.title: Bangladesh’s education budget is building third floor on crumbling foundation
item.description: On June 11, 2026, the government announced a record nearly BDT 1.36 trillion education budget for FY 2026-27 budget — up 56.6 percent from last year. Policies rarely fail at the announcement stage; they fail during implementation. This budget offers high hope of narrowing that implementation gap.

item.title: Canal re-excavation will reduce floods, droughts, says Disaster Management Minister
item.description: The restored canals will create opportunities for agriculture during the dry season, fish farming, and tree plantation along canal banks.

item.title: World Bank helps Bangladesh strengthen its banking sector
item.description: The Financial Sector Support Project II aims to bolster the deposit protection system to protect small depositors and build Bangladesh Bank’s supervisory capacity and systems.

item.title: CCEA gives in-principle nod to 7 packages under DSCC canal restoration project
item.description: According to the approved procurement strategy, the works will be implemented through the Direct Procurement Method (DPM).

item.title: Nearly 19,000 government accounts remain outside treasury framework
item.description: Since these accounts operate outside direct oversight, it has become difficult to monitor the transparency of transactions and the wastage of funds conducted through them.

item.title: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman arrives in Beijing
item.description: The prime minister was accorded a guard of honour and a ceremonial welcome. From there, a motorcade took him to the Diaoyutai State Guesthouse.

item.title: BDT 476bn metro project set for next ECNEC meeting
item.description: While the scheme has been repeatedly tabled at ECNEC sessions since 2022, fiscal overruns and volatile foreign financing prospects historically stalled its approval.

item.title: Oman opens temporary Strait of Hormuz shipping routes, says no tolls will be charged
item.description: Oman said ‌navigational ⁠safety remained the overriding priority and that a gradual, controlled movement of vessel traffic was required because of an elevated risk of collisions.

item.title: 86% of factory closures linked to weak orders, financial distress
item.description: Experts urge targeted support for closed factories, warning that many older shutdowns would require extensive rebuilding.

item.title: PM heads to Beijing after WEF meeting in Dalian
item.description: The prime minister departed from Dalian North Railway Station at around 2 pm (China local time), accompanied by his spouse, Dr Zubaida Rahman.

item.title: Gold price cut by BDT 5,482 a bhori as local rates ease
item.description: Wednesday’s decrease marked the 80th gold price revision so far in 2026, with cuts now totalling 39 against 40 increases and one VAT adjustment.

item.title: Israel smuggled Starlink systems into Iran, former PM says
item.description: Iran has previously accused Israel and ​the United States of smuggling in the devices to undermine its security. Starlink is not licensed to operate in Iran but Musk has previously said the service is active there.

item.title: Rubio kicks off Middle East trip as allies seek answers on Iran
item.description: Rubio is undertaking his first high-level diplomatic mission on the agreement reached last week to end the four-month-old U.S.-Israeli ​war with Iran.

item.title: PM joins WEF annual meet in Dalian city of China
item.description: On the sidelines of the session, the prime minister discussed strengthening bilateral relations and issues of mutual interest with heads of government from different countries of the world.

item.title: Five firms buy data for Bangladesh offshore licensing round
item.description: Five companies have bought geological data packages for the Bay of Bengal licensing round, but none has yet purchased tender documents as firms assess the revised fiscal terms before the November 30 bidding deadline.

item.title: US, Iran at odds on nuclear inspections, frozen assets in deal to end war
item.description: The two countries, which held a first round of negotiations in Switzerland that ended on Monday, also offered conflicting accounts about financial incentives for Iran, control of the Strait of Hormuz, and Israel’s parallel war in Lebanon.

item.title: Nine stranded at border after alleged BSF pushback
item.description: Border Guard Bangladesh say a patrol blocked the group from entering the country near Naogaon and left them at the zero line.

item.title: Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six World Cups with two goals against Uzbekistan
item.description: Ronaldo became the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments by getting two goals in Portugal’s 5-0 win over Uzbekistan.

item.title: PM urges global leaders to turn climate pledges into action
item.description: The prime minister said Bangladesh believes that climate action is not a cost, adding that the country sees this as a much-needed investment for prosperity, stability and a shared future.

item.title: UN chief calls on AI firms to come clean on environmental costs
item.description: The rapid development of data centres ​globally to fuel the AI revolution has drawn scrutiny from environmental groups for their ​high energy and water use and lack of transparency.

item.title: Iran says card-based banking hit by cyberattack on three lenders
item.description: This had prompted a temporary suspension of all card-related operations at the three ​banks to prevent further unauthorised access, the ​company told state TV, with cybersecurity ⁠teams working to restore normal operations.

item.title: Seven DAGs, eleven AAGs resign simultaneously
item.description: The officials submitted their resignation letters on Tuesday afternoon.

item.title: Railway Minister inaugurates railway tree plantation programme
item.description: The minister said that the railway bridge over the Dhaleshwari River had been constructed under the Padma Rail Link Project.

item.title: PM seeks coordinated WEF measures for climate-vulnerable countries
item.description: The prime minister noted the country’s efforts to restore water flow, reduce flood risks, and protect the environment through the re-excavation of nearly 20,000 kilometres of rivers and canals.

item.title: Bangladesh could lift exports to $150bn, commerce minister says
item.description: Referring to the country’s fulfilment of all LDC graduation criteria, the minister stressed that the main challenges were surviving global competition after graduation and building the capacity to advance to the next stage.

item.title: Foreign debt repayments top $4bn in 11 months, ERD data show
item.description: Rising repayments and weaker disbursements narrowed the gap between foreign financing inflows and outflows during the first 11 months of the 2025–26 fiscal year.

item.title: DP World seeks 30-year operating rights on NCT based on a 15-year financial model
item.description: The Chittagong Port Authority has formed a 12-member special support team, comprising experienced officials from various departments of the port, to assist the main negotiation committee with the necessary technical, operational, financial, and administrative information, data and recommendations.

item.title: Special taskforce to be formed to strengthen dengue prevention, response activities
item.description: LGRD Minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir called on all relevant institutions to work in a coordinated manner to prevent a dengue outbreak.

item.title: Local govt elections may begin on Sept-Oct, says PM’s adviser
item.description: Union parishad polls are likely to be held first, followed by municipalities, upazilas, district councils and city corporations, the prime minister’s adviser on information told a briefing in Dhaka.

item.title: Awami League ban decision a matter for court, says PM's adviser
item.description: The adviser said the party’s ban on activities under the Anti-Terrorism Act 2009 would remain in place while it remained under judicial process.

item.title: Renewable energy push to expand solar systems as part of ‘welfare state’, says chief whip
item.description: The chief whip said about 73 percent of rural people in Bangladesh were already benefiting from some form of solar systems, describing it as a major achievement.

item.title: Government to launch first short-term sukuk
item.description: The central bank said the nine-month government investment sukuk, structured under an ijara lease contract, will fund the IRIDP-2.

item.title: Keir Starmer went from election landslide to downfall after his supporters deserted him
item.description: On Monday, Starmer said in an emotional statement that he was stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party, though he will remain caretaker prime minister until a new Labour leader is chosen in the coming weeks.

item.title: ‘Pure’ New Zealand chases gold as record prices burnish allure
item.description: Miners see potential in the underexplored nation as its government acts to boost jobs amid near decade-high unemployment and weakening business sentiment.

item.title: SpaceX turns to bond market to raise capital, reports $100.8 billion cash
item.description: SpaceX has increased ​spending on AI infrastructure and the development of its next-generation Starship rocket, investments that have ​weighed on profitability despite strong growth at its Starlink satellite internet business.

item.title: Undoing the ‘tangled nest’ of Iran sanctions won’t be easy or quick
item.description: Under a 14-point memorandum of understanding signed by the U.S. and Iran last week, Washington is to start abolishing all types of sanctions using a schedule to be forged in a final deal within 60 days, a period that can be extended.

item.title: New extortion networks vie for control of ‘jhut’ trade
item.description: The lucrative trade in garment scraps worth billions of taka has become the focus of fresh turf battles and extortion networks, prompting calls for regulation and transparent trading mechanisms.

item.title: ‘MV Banglar Joyjatra’ resumes voyage after clearing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On April 18, Iran’s navy denied the Banglar Joyjatra permission to transit the strait, citing security and strategic considerations, leaving the vessel unable to leave the Persian Gulf region.

item.title: US waives Iran sanctions after talks; Lebanon fighting abates
item.description: The two ​sides, trying to build on the interim deal they signed last week, agreed a roadmap towards a permanent agreement within 60 days at the talks.

item.title: China rolls out red carpet as PM Tarique Rahman arrives in Dalian
item.description: The prime minister will have a busy schedule in Dalian before travelling to Beijing, the capital of China, where the main events of his official visit will begin.

item.title: BPC incurred BDT 170.39 billion in losses due to Middle East conflict
item.description: The minister noted that if international fuel prices decline to a more tolerable level in the future, the government will consider reducing the prices in the domestic market as well.

item.title: Law Minister emphasises raising social awareness to protect senior citizens
item.description: The minister said that alongside the state, families and society must also play a responsible role in ensuring their well-being.

item.title: Khulna Medical’s Cancer Department exists in name only, fails to deliver expected services
item.description: Patients and their relatives also allege that there is a severe shortage of chemotherapy services. While chemotherapy is available free of charge or at a nominal cost in government hospitals in Dhaka, patients at KMCH must pay substantial amounts for the same service.

item.title: US and Iran make ‘encouraging progress’ at talks although tension remains
item.description: The talks began under the terms of the memorandum of understanding reached last week to extend a tenuous ceasefire from April for at least another 60 days.

item.title: Recoverable gas reserves stand at 7.63 Tcf, minister tells Parliament
item.description: The minister also highlighted a series of government initiatives aimed at bolstering energy security, curbing import dependence and lifting domestic capacity.

item.title: Army to be deployed at four cities, two districts amid security measures
item.description: The home minister said the government remains vigilant over potential attempts to disrupt public order.

item.title: Funds sent abroad for higher education more than triple over six years
item.description: This substantial outflow of funds exceeds the total allocation provided by the University Grants Commission (UGC) to the country’s 57 public universities.

item.title: Bangladesh, Malaysia advance strategic partnership across sectors
item.description: The commitment came on Monday during Prime Minister Tarique Rahman’s official visit to Malaysia at the invitation of Malaysian Prime Minister Dato’ Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

item.title: UK PM Starmer says he will resign
item.description: Whoever replaces Starmer will become Britain’s seventh prime minister since the Brexit vote to leave the European Union which took place 10 years ago this week.

item.title: Bangladesh aims to cut business start-up time to 14 days, says commerce minister
item.description: Under the target, a company could ideally open a letter of credit to import machinery on the 15th day, the minister told a debriefing session in Dhaka.

item.title: UK’s Starmer expected to set out exit timetable as Burnham waits in the wings
item.description: Less than two years after Starmer won a landslide election victory that promised to end ‌Britain’s chaotic politics, one source said the prime minister had spent the weekend considering whether to step aside or fight a leadership contest.

item.title: Qatar brings LNG tankers into Hormuz despite shipping slowdown
item.description: The tankers - Wadi Al Sail, Mekaines, Al Sadd and Mesaimeer - were entering the strait via the Iranian route for the first time since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran started.

item.title: PM urges Malaysia to open labour market, hire more Bangladeshi workers
item.description: Bangladeshi workers, students, professionals, and entrepreneurs in Malaysia serve as an important bridge between the two countries, the prime minister said, adding that their contributions benefit both their economies and societies.

item.title: SDF faces scrutiny over spending, governance as state debt burden grows
item.description: The Financial Institutions Division is investigating allegations of procurement irregularities, misuse of funds and governance failures at the Social Development Foundation, which has spent more than BDT 64.8 billion since 2000.

item.title: Gold price rises BDT 4,432 per bhori after two straight drops
item.description: The new price, an increase of BDT 4,432, took effect from 10 am, BAJUS said in a statement.

item.title: Cape Verde gets 1st goal of tournament and holds Uruguay to 2-2 draw
item.description: The tiny island nation that stunned tournament favorite Spain last week did it again against Uruguay — a two-time World Cup champion — on Sunday, coming from behind for a 2-2 draw.

item.title: Salah scores and Egypt downs New Zealand 3-1 for a first World Cup victory
item.description: Mohamed Salah scored the go-ahead goal and Egypt went on to defeat New Zealand 3-1 on Sunday night.

item.title: Oil prices slip in international market after US-Iran conclude talks in Switzerland
item.description: Brent crude fell $1.53, or 1.90 percent, to $79.04 a barrel by 0656 GMT. Prices had earlier climbed to $82.30 at the start of trading.

item.title: US and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say
item.description: The parties agreed to a mechanism to end the fighting in Lebanon and opened a communications line to help ensure safe passages for commercial ships through the contested strait, a statement said.

item.title: Tarique Rahman holds one-to-one talks with Anwar Ibrahim
item.description: The two prime ministers were scheduled to hold a joint press conference after the bilateral meeting.

item.title: Red-carpet welcome for Prime Minister Tarique Rahman in Kuala Lumpur
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim will formally receive the Bangladeshi prime minister on Monday morning at his office in Putrajaya.

item.title: Environment Minister calls for united efforts to control noise pollution
item.description: The minister emphasised that effective noise pollution control requires the cooperation of government and private institutions, relevant agencies, and the general public.

item.title: Two more die from dengue
item.description: With these deaths, the number of dengue-related fatalities this month has risen to four, while the total death toll this year has reached nine.

item.title: Five Bangladeshis killed in road accident in Qatar
item.description: In a condolence message, the state minister for the Ministry of Expatriates’ Welfare and Overseas Employment said that the sudden and tragic deaths of these remittance earners, who contribute significantly to the country’s economy, are deeply painful.

item.title: Govt to recruit 100,000 health workers and 25,000 midwives
item.description: PM Special Assistant on Health Affairs said the government aims to transform the healthcare system from a treatment-centred model to a prevention-centred one by strengthening primary healthcare services.

item.title: Sylhet DC Sarwar Alam withdrawn from post
item.description: He has been withdrawn from his previous post and attached to the Ministry of Public Administration as a Deputy Secretary.

item.title: Minister calls for science-based initiatives to keep youth free from drugs
item.description: Emphasising the roles of government, educational institutions, families, and social organisations in building a drug-free society, the minister said that a social movement must be formed to eliminate drugs from the country.

item.title: Govt strengthening online one-stop investment services: Commerce Minister
item.description: The minister stated that the government is working to make the licensing and approval process easier, faster and more integrated so that investors do not have to move from one office to another.

item.title: Europe swelters under heatwave, France restricts alcohol consumption
item.description: After a crisis meeting, Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu ⁠pre-emptively banned alcohol consumption on Sunday at the annual Fete de la Musique festivals and other ​public events to be held in those 35 regions on Sunday.

item.title: Sada Pathor closes indefinitely amid flash flood risks
item.description: The Companiganj UNO said once the water current subsides and weather conditions stabilise, the decision on reopening the tourist spot will be taken.

item.title: Finance minister signals move towards market-based, alternative financing
item.description: The current FY26 budget will spend about BDT 1.25 trillion on interest payments, the finance minister said, limiting the government’s fiscal space.

item.title: Telegram loses bid to overturn India’s temporary blocking of the app
item.description: The government is “empowered ... to issue directions for blocking the public access to Telegram,” Delhi ​High Court Justice Tejas Karia said in his ruling.

item.title: Global indicator of inefficiency becomes major revenue source for Ctg Port
item.description: Stakeholders believe this situation reflects the long-standing structural limitations of the port and customs systems.

item.title: PM Tarique Rahman departs Dhaka for Malaysia
item.description: The Malaysia tour is expected to focus on trade expansion, labour market cooperation and investment opportunities.

item.title: Report says UK PM Starmer ready to quit, but source says he is still focused on the job
item.description: A government source said Starmer remained focused on his job and pointed to previous statements ​he has made to that effect.

item.title: Top-level US-Iran peace talks to begin at Swiss resort with Hormuz in spotlight
item.description: A halt to fighting in Lebanon was one of the conditions for starting U.S.-Iranian talks on Tehran’s nuclear programme and other ​issues. But Lebanese civil defence officials said Israeli strikes had killed 20 people on Saturday, hours after a truce took effect.

item.title: Five youths stranded for eight days on Kurigram border zero line
item.description: The incident began on June 14 when India’s Border Security Force (BSF) attempted to push six people at Goytapara and three youths at Bhondurchar, according to local accounts.

item.title: 12th Int’l Yoga Day celebrated in Dhaka with healthy ageing theme
item.description: The event followed a series of pre-Yoga Day activities held across Bangladesh in collaboration with educational, media and cultural institutions.

item.title: CAAB launches national civil aviation master plan
item.description: The proposed CAMP will serve as a comprehensive national roadmap for the aviation sector for the next 20 to 30 years and beyond.

item.title: PM heading to Malaysia and China on six-day visit
item.description: Officials expect the prime minister’s first overseas trip since taking office in February this year to yield a broad package of agreements as Bangladesh seeks closer economic and diplomatic ties with two key Asian partners.

item.title: US Vice President JD Vance arrives in Switzerland to launch talks with Iran on its nuclear program
item.description: Top U.S. and Iranian negotiators are in a 60-day sprint to reach an agreement on the technical details that hold massive implications for the world economy and global security.

item.title: Government borrowing from banks triples while private-sector credit flow falls
item.description: Economists and business leaders warn that heavy government reliance on bank financing is worsening a prolonged credit squeeze on private enterprises.

item.title: Undav scores twice as Germany beats Ivory Coast 2-1, advances to World Cup knockout phase
item.description: Undav scored twice as Germany defeated Ivory Coast 2-1 on Saturday and clinched a spot in the knockout phase at the World Cup.

item.title: Pink boots are everywhere at the World Cup as the color becomes a soccer star
item.description: Pink itself does not make players perform any better, but shoe company executives consider it a mindset.

item.title: Curacao’s Eloy Room makes 15 saves, sends island nation to 0-0 draw with Ecuador in World Cup
item.description: The 37-year-old Room, whose shutout of Jamaica last November sent Curacao to its first World Cup, bounced back in historic fashion from a 7-1 loss to Germany.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 20 in Lebanon hours after ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's National News Agency reported that Israeli warplanes and drones had struck multiple locations in southern ​Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley.

item.title: US disputes Iranian claims about closing Strait of Hormuz as negotiators head to Switzerland
item.description: Although the U.S. and Iran had agreed to a 60-day ceasefire while negotiations take place, Tehran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on Saturday declared the Strait of Hormuz shut.

item.title: Patenga terminal adds 4 custom gantry cranes
item.description: The supplier will install and commission the cranes at the jetty, with operations expected to begin by mid-July.

item.title: An Israeli strike hits a Gaza City apartment, killing 2 children, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: The Israeli military didn’t immediately issue a statement, but said it was looking into the incident.

item.title: PM calls for united efforts to harness maritime potential
item.description: The prime minister said in the global reality of the twenty-first century, the ocean is not merely a part of geographical boundaries; rather, it is one of the principal foundations of economic growth, trade connectivity, energy security, environmental balance and national strategic capability.

item.title: Govt to form panels to review CPI, GDP methodologies: State Minister
item.description: The state minister for planning said the review aims to improve the accuracy, transparency and credibility of official economic statistics.

item.title: Tea production climbs 130% in strong start to 2026
item.description: Growers expanded output after a recovery in auction prices improved profitability, reversing two years of production shortfalls.

item.title: US, Iran envoys head for talks, Israeli strikes continue after ceasefire
item.description: Just hours after a ceasefire took effect in Lebanon between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, Israeli air strikes and drone attacks killed at least five people in the south on Saturday, Lebanese state media said.

item.title: Bangladesh renews call for Rohingya repatriation at UN
item.description: Speaking at a UN briefing, Bangladesh Permanent Representative to the United Nations Ambassador Salahuddin Noman Chowdhury said the Rohingya crisis originated in Myanmar and its durable solution must also be found there.

item.title: Animal activists in Dhaka plan human chain to protest stray dog culling calls
item.description: Bangladesh’s officially approved method for managing stray dogs is Catch-Neuter-Vaccinate-Release, an internationally recognised approach, the organisers said.

item.title: Dhaka, Beijing to sign 15-17 instruments during PM’s visit, says foreign secretary
item.description: The prime minister is scheduled to travel to China on Monday afternoon at the invitation of Chinese Premier Li Qiang.

item.title: Border district Thakurgaon carries growing geopolitical relevance
item.description: Thakurgaon’s proximity to India’s Siliguri Corridor and its history of producing national political figures have given the north-western district an outsized role in Bangladesh’s political and strategic landscape.

item.title: Pentagon chief’s review appears out of step with what NATO allies are already doing
item.description: Hegseth does not attend many NATO meetings, and he left Thursday’s gathering of defense ministers early. But his first major speech to the allies in February 2025 and his follow-up this week were memorable.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank moves to tighten SWIFT security with AML tools
item.description: The central bank is adding anti-money laundering tools to its SWIFT defences. The technology automatically suspends suspect transactions the moment they arise, placing all international transfers under heightened scrutiny.

item.title: Paraguay’s Miguel Almiron becomes 1st player red-carded at the World Cup for covering his mouth
item.description: The infraction came during first-half stoppage time in the Group D match when Almiron and Mert Mulder exchanged words following a foul near midfield.

item.title: US reaches World Cup knockout round and wins its group with 2nd straight victory, 2-0 over Australia
item.description: As the Group D winner, the U.S. will play a round-of-32 match on July 1 in Santa Clara, California, against a third-place group finisher.

item.title: PM’s Malaysia visit to prioritise labour pact changes, lower migration costs
item.description: Bangladesh will seek changes to an existing memorandum of understanding that officials say has hindered transparent recruitment, as Prime Minister Tarique Rahman travels to Malaysia this week.

item.title: Matheus Cunha scores 2 goals as Brazil eliminates Haiti from World Cup with 3-0 win
item.description: Haiti, the Western Hemisphere’s poorest nation that qualified for the World Cup for the first time since 1974, became the first team guaranteed not to reach the knockout round of the expanded 48-team tournament.

item.title: Bangladesh seeks deeper UN Women cooperation
item.description: The state minister made the appeal during a bilateral meeting in New York with Sima Bahous, the executive director of UN Women.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold prices for second straight day
item.description: The new rate of BDT 226,340 per bhori, including value-added tax, took effect at 10 am local time, BAJUS said in a statement.

item.title: Ukrainian drone makers target Asia as Taiwan tensions spur demand
item.description: Ukrainian defense firms like UFORCE, Skyeton and General Cherry hope to find production partners in Japan, ​an industrial heavyweight that this year cast off longstanding restrictions on arms exports.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni says Trump ‘totally invented’ story that she begged him for photo
item.description: Underscoring how much Trump’s ​comments have angered Meloni’s government, Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani pulled out of a visit to the U.S. next week.

item.title: Iranian Guards’ business empire to win big if US sanctions lifted
item.description: The interim deal announced this week will allow waivers on sanctioned oil sales while any more comprehensive agreement struck in the coming period could lift all other sanctions and ​give Iran access to a $300-billion reconstruction fund.

item.title: Bangladesh lose T20I series to Australia
item.description: The Tigers lost the three-match series 2-0.

item.title: Kuakata beach losing shoreline, scenic beauty due to erosion, mismanagement
item.description: Kuakata beach, in particular, has experienced severe erosion since 2010. Since then, more than 2,000 metres of beachfront have been lost to the sea.

item.title: Bangladeshi deposits in Swiss banks rise by 41.5% in one year
item.description: If a Bangladeshi deposits funds using another nationality or conceals their identity, such funds are not included in this data, according to the Swiss central bank.

item.title: China tightens indium export checks as AI demand increases
item.description: China produces nearly 70 percent of the world’s indium, a byproduct of zinc refining mostly used in displays and solder, but also the raw material for making indium phosphide, used to make high-speed optical ​chips for AI data centres.

item.title: India’s RMZ to ramp up data centre capacity with $35 billion push, exec says
item.description: The Bengaluru-headquartered company, which currently has 250 megawatts of capacity, is in the final stages of discussions for three data centre projects that would take its total capacity to more than 1 gigawatt, Deepak Chhabria, president of RMZ Infrastructure, told Reuters in an interview on Thursday.

item.title: Gold and silver prices fall in domestic market
item.description: In a notice issued this morning, BAJUS said the new prices came into effect at 10:00 am.

item.title: Drone strikes beyond the battlefield pump up market for technology to repel them
item.description: The field is drawing billions of dollars of investment, moving well beyond military use to sectors including energy, shipping, data ​centres, hotels and airports.

item.title: New govt medical colleges approved before resolving crises at existing ones
item.description: Experts argue that deficiencies in fundamental facilities create significant obstacles to producing competent physicians in the medical sector, adding that shortcomings are preventing the sector from adequately preparing for future challenges.

item.title: Puppetry pioneer Mustafa Monwar under intensive care in Dhaka
item.description: The artist earned national recognition for his creative children’s programming on the state broadcaster Bangladesh Television (BTV), which shaped the childhoods of successive generations.

item.title: Padma erosion threatens Daulatdia ferry jetties; hundreds of families at risk
item.description: Locals said geo-bag dumping during the monsoon had not fully prevented erosion, arguing that limited preventive work in the dry season reduced its effectiveness.

item.title: Luis Díaz sparks Colombia to a 3-1 win over Uzbekistan in its World Cup opener
item.description: With the White Wolves’ defeat, three of the four debuting teams at this year’s World Cup lost their first match.

item.title: Kane draws level with Lineker as England outgun Croatia
item.description: The Group L meeting carried heavy expectations long before kickoff, given the history between the sides, with Croatia’s extra-time victory over England in the 2018 ‌World Cup semi-finals adding an extra edge to the occasion.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal held to a 1-1 draw by Congo at the World Cup
item.description: Playing in front of a crowd of 68,777 spectators at NRG Stadium, which was mostly filled with Portugal supporters, Congo earned its first point at the World Cup after losing all three of its previous matches.

item.title: US awards $500 million to Nvidia-backed SandboxAQ for finding new chipmaking materials
item.description: The award is part of President Donald Trump’s move to allocate research funds under the CHIPS Act, an effort that previously funded a $150 million investment in new chip manufacturing tools and a $2 billion investment in quantum computing.

item.title: Bangladesh’s budget: The real deficit is institutional
item.description: The debate surrounding the FY 2026–27 budget has focused heavily on the fiscal deficit, revenue targets, and growth projections. But it overlooks a fundamental question: can Bangladesh translate public expenditure into public value?

item.title: Musk says SpaceX could bring $1 trillion in revenue by 2030
item.description: SpaceX on Friday became the sixth-largest U.S. firm, cementing Musk’s status as the world’s first ​trillionaire.

item.title: In Brazil’s Cerrado region, Indigenous fire practices reshape wildfire strategy
item.description: The Xerente live in the Cerrado, a savanna in central and northern Brazil. Every year, villages face the threat of large-scale forest fires, a danger likely to worsen with the arrival of El Niño, a phenomenon that prolongs drought and pushes regional temperatures higher.

item.title: “Bangladesh’s first recycling project for waste management to be launched in Khulna”
item.description: “Khulna City Corporation and the Asian Development Bank are jointly implementing the project with financial support from the latter. The project has an allocated budget of BDT 750 million.”

item.title: “LPG now a clean, safe and reliable fuel source for millions of Bangladeshi households”
item.description: “Initially, LPG was seen by many as an alternative fuel. Today, it has become the primary cooking fuel for millions of households across the country. Consumers now place greater importance on its convenience, cleanliness, safety and reliability.”

item.title: “At Petromax, safety is not just a compliance requirement but a core responsibility”
item.description: “At Petromax, we view safety not as a compliance requirement but as a core responsibility. By embedding safety into every stage of our operations — from import to end-user delivery — we aim to build trust, protect lives and ensure the sustainable growth of the LPG sector in Bangladesh.”

item.title: “United Aygaz has made the LPG market more competitive”
item.description: “Aygaz’s 65 years of global experience and international sourcing network. We believe that with the entry of United Aygaz, the market has become even more competitive. Competition keeps our competitors sharp, and their strength keeps us sharp as well. It is healthy — good for consumers, distributors and the companies themselves.”

item.title: War as strategy: How Iran’s authoritarian regime instrumentalises conflict for domestic control
item.description: Authoritarian regimes are structurally constrained by an inherent deficit of democratic legitimacy, given the absence of free, competitive, and institutionalised electoral processes. Consequently, they must rely on alternative sources of legitimation, including nationalist narratives, ideological framing, performance-based claims, and coercive capacity.

item.title: Rethinking fiscal governance: A blueprint for Bangladesh’s tax future
item.description: Fiscal reform in Bangladesh must go beyond revenue targets. It is not enough to chase numbers; the system must be rebuilt to foster trust and voluntary compliance. By embracing transparency, democracy, prestige, agility, and empathy, the country can unlock a new wave of civic participation.

item.title: Beyond relief: rethinking the Rohingya response
item.description: The Rohingya response is no longer only a humanitarian challenge. It is also a question of long-term policy, regional stability, and shared responsibility. Continuing to meet immediate needs remains essential, but it must be complemented by forward-looking approaches that expand opportunities and reduce dependency over time.

item.title: “A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation”
item.description: “The fundamental weakness is that we still treat the budget essentially as an income-expenditure ledger. A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation. It should spell out which sectors the government will prioritise over the coming year…”

item.title: Runa Laila to receive ‘Minar-E-Dilli’ honour
item.description: The honour will be presented to her at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival in Delhi.

item.title: Asha Bhosle, one of India’s most versatile Bollywood singers, passes away at 92
item.description: She was admitted to the hospital on late Saturday with a chest infection and exhaustion, her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle said in a social media post.

item.title: An extraordinary story of survival retold on screen
item.description: Each day brought uncertainty and the film brings that experience to the big screen.

item.title: A major milestone for Bangladesh’s cinema industry
item.description: Competing against 11 other features in the Big Screen Competition at the International Film Festival Rotterdam (IFFR), the Bangladeshi film “Master” has earned its place among the world’s most prestigious film festivals.

item.title: Bangladeshi folk film ‘Behula Dorodi’ to screen at international festivals
item.description: Jahidul Islam, the producer, said he aims to establish Bengali cinema as a viable commercial proposition internationally while preserving “dying folk traditions” for a new generation through the film.
